hypothesis: If we compute last-layer embeddings for the same sets of molecules, materials, and proteins across diverse scientific foundation models (including geometric, graph, sequence, and language architectures), then these representations will exhibit significant cross-model alignment (high CKNNA and IDCor with low bidirectional information imbalance) even on out-of-distribution data because independent training on matter drives convergence to a shared latent manifold capturing common physical–chemical factors.
